# HTTP Action Bot

> Trigger safe REST API requests from WhatsApp commands and map JSON responses back to chat — connect
> WhatsApp to an existing HTTPS API without webhook middleware.

![type: extension](https://img.shields.io/badge/type-extension-blue.svg)
![license: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-green.svg)
![built for OpenWA](https://img.shields.io/badge/OpenWA-%E2%89%A5%200.8.7-25D366.svg)

## Features

- **Command triggers** — each action matches an `exact` or `prefix` command (case-insensitive by
  default); the first matching action wins and its trailing arguments are parsed (a double-quoted run is
  kept as one argument).
- **Fixed-origin requests** — `GET` and JSON `POST` to a single HTTPS `baseUrl`; the path is
  server-relative and every interpolated segment is URL-encoded, so a message can never change the host
  or inject a path segment.
- **Auth** — none, Bearer token, or API-key header (header name configurable).
- **Templated replies** — map the JSON response into a reply with `{{response.field}}`, plus separate
  `notFoundTemplate` and `errorTemplate`. Prototype keys are rejected and POST bodies are JSON-escaped.
- **Direct-chat by default** — group chats are ignored unless `respondInGroups` is enabled.
- **Reliable** — one request per message; idempotent across WhatsApp redelivery (storage-backed dedup),
  per-chat cooldown, and an off-dispatch handler so a slow upstream never stalls the inbound hook.

## Actions

`actions` is a JSON array; each entry is `{ "id", "match": { "type", "value", "caseSensitive"? }, "request": { "method", "path", "query"?, "headers"?, "bodyTemplate"? }, "replyTemplate", "notFoundTemplate"?, "errorTemplate"? }`:

```json
[
  {
    "id": "check-order",
    "match": { "type": "prefix", "value": "cek-order " },
    "request": { "method": "GET", "path": "/orders/{{args.0}}" },
    "replyTemplate": "Order {{response.orderId}}: {{response.status}}\nTracking: {{response.trackingNumber}}",
    "notFoundTemplate": "Order not found.",
    "errorTemplate": "Order status service is unavailable. Please try again later."
  }
]
```

Template variables: `args.0`, `args.1`, … plus `message.id`, `message.body`, `chat.id`, `sender.id`,
`sender.phone`, `sender.name`, `session.id`, and `response.<path>` (the parsed JSON body).

## Setup

1. Have an **HTTPS API** you want to call from WhatsApp — its origin goes in `baseUrl`.
2. Have OpenWA **≥ 0.8.7** running with a logged-in WhatsApp session.
3. Install and enable the plugin (see [Install](#install)), then define your actions
   (see [Configuration](#configuration)).

## Install

```bash
node package.mjs http-action    # produces http-action.zip at the repo root

curl -X POST "https://your-openwa-host/plugins/install" \
  -H "X-API-Key: <ADMIN_API_KEY>" -F "file=@http-action.zip"

curl -X PUT "https://your-openwa-host/plugins/http-action/config" \
  -H "X-API-Key: <ADMIN_API_KEY>" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{ "config": { "baseUrl": "https://erp.example.com", "actions": "[{\"id\":\"check-order\",\"match\":{\"type\":\"prefix\",\"value\":\"cek-order \"},\"request\":{\"method\":\"GET\",\"path\":\"/orders/{{args.0}}\"},\"replyTemplate\":\"Order {{response.orderId}}: {{response.status}}\"}]" } }'

curl -X POST "https://your-openwa-host/plugins/http-action/enable" \
  -H "X-API-Key: <ADMIN_API_KEY>"
```

Or download the packaged `.zip` from [Releases](https://github.com/rmyndharis/OpenWA-plugins/releases)
and upload it in the dashboard **Plugins → Install** (or the **Catalog** tab).

## Configuration

| Key | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | -------- | ------- | ----------- |
| `baseUrl` | yes | — | HTTPS origin (e.g. `https://erp.example.com`). Its host is auto-added to the outbound allowlist. Must be non-empty. |
| `authType` | no | `none` | `none` · `bearer` · `apikey` |
| `authToken` | no | — | Bearer token or API key (required when `authType` is not `none`); masked `***`. |
| `apiKeyHeader` | no | `X-API-Key` | Header name used when `authType=apikey`. |
| `respondInGroups` | no | `false` | Whether to act on group chats. |
| `timeoutMs` | no | `3000` | Upstream request timeout in ms (min 500). |
| `cooldownSeconds` | no | `3` | Min seconds between replies per chat; 0 = always. |
| `actions` | yes | — | JSON array of actions (see **Actions**). |

## Compatibility

Status is **development**. It targets OpenWA **≥ 0.8.7** (`allowConfigHosts` + `conversation:send`);
`testedOpenWAVersion` is not yet set — the plugin needs install and runtime testing on a real OpenWA
instance before being marked `beta`. Live config edits apply on the next inbound message (config is
re-read per event).

## Security

- **Fixed origin.** `baseUrl` must be HTTPS with no embedded credentials, query string, or fragment, and
  is declared in `allowConfigHosts` (a code-side default is forbidden — the net gate resolves the allowed
  host from the raw config). The request path is server-relative only (protocol-relative `//`, absolute
  URLs, fragments, and control chars are rejected) and each interpolated segment is URL-encoded, so a
  message cannot change the host or inject a path segment; `..` traversal segments are rejected too.
- **No arbitrary code.** No `eval`, no free regex, no loops. Prototype keys (`__proto__`/`constructor`/
  `prototype`) are rejected in template paths; POST bodies are JSON-escaped and re-parsed before send;
  dangerous headers (hop-by-hop, `x-forwarded-*`) are blocked and CR/LF is rejected in rendered header
  values. Response bodies are capped at 256 KiB.
- **Reliability.** Storage-backed idempotency (fail-closed check plus a marker written only after a
  successful reply) so a redelivered message id never double-fires and a transient send failure retries;
  in-memory per-chat cooldown (fail-open). The handler returns `{ continue: true }` immediately and
  floats the fetch/render/send, so a slow upstream never stalls the inbound hook.
- **Secrets.** `authToken` is marked `secret` in `configSchema`, so the dashboard masks it and preserves
  the stored value on save.
- **Redirects.** Cross-host redirect and private-IP filtering are the host proxy's responsibility —
  `ctx.net.fetch` exposes no `redirect` option — so the plugin makes no redirect guarantee.
